tactical_shape: Bielsa's side dominated possession but were repeatedly exposed by Cape Verde's direct running and transitions.
Pushed frantically for the winner after the equaliser, with a very open, disorganised attack that risked being countered at the end.
standout_players: Fernando Muslera — veteran keeper struggled on Pina's free-kick and was in no man’s land for Varela’s goal.
Maximiliano Araújo — scored Uruguay’s first equaliser with a close-range header.
Agustín Canobbio — finished Uruguay’s second goal from close range, though later lacked calmness when through on goal.
Federico Valverde — came close with a free-kick narrowly over the bar.
Rodrigo Bentancur — header deflected off Cabral and onto a post before the equaliser chance.
fatigue_injuries: Marcelo Bielsa said Ronald Araújo will not return from injury for the next game.
home_read.momentum: Highly disorganised and under serious pressure; Uruguay’s tournament hopes are in jeopardy and they must beat Spain.

tactical_shape: Fearless, direct approach with quick running and electric pace, especially through Garry Rodrigues.
Held firm defensively for long spells and stayed resilient under pressure, even when hemmed in late on.
standout_players: Kevin Pina — scored Cape Verde’s maiden World Cup goal from a free-kick.
Garry Rodrigues — his touch and pace repeatedly troubled Uruguay.
Hélio Varela — scored the equaliser and showed great composure.
Steven Moreira — produced an outstanding block to deny Uruguay the lead.
Sidny Cabral — involved in creating panic with a cross-cum-shot and was unlucky with the deflected equaliser sequence.
fatigue_injuries: Arcanjo was assisted with cramp by Federico Viñas.
Vozinha spilled the ball for the disallowed Uruguay goal but no injury was reported.
away_read.momentum: The underdogs continue to inspire belief; Cape Verde are fearless, resilient and in strong position to chase qualification.
